task_completion.description: Verify that a .dockerignore file was created in the project directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing 'ls -la' listing a .dockerignore file, or the file open in a text editor/IDE tree view.

task_completion.description: Verify that the .dockerignore file contains the required exclusions to prevent cache invalidation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of .dockerignore visible in a text editor or via a 'cat' command in the terminal, explicitly showing '.git' and '__pycache__'.

task_completion.description: Verify that system package installations (e.g., apt-get) are placed before the main source code copy instruction.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dockerfile contents visible in an editor or terminal showing 'RUN apt-get update' and 'apt-get install' occurring before 'COPY . .' or similar full-source copy commands.

task_completion.description: Verify that Python dependencies are installed before the main source code is copied.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dockerfile contents showing 'COPY requirements.txt ...' followed by 'RUN pip install ...' occurring before the main 'COPY . .' instruction.

task_completion.description: Verify that the main source code copy instruction is placed after all dependency installations to maximize caching.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dockerfile contents showing 'COPY . .' (or equivalent source copy) placed near the end of the file, strictly after the apt and pip installation steps.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Docker image was successfully built with the correct tag.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution and successful completion of 'docker build -t build-project:optimized .' with standard Docker build output.

task_completion.description: Verify that the optimized container is running and accessible on port 5000.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a 'docker run' command mapping port 5000 (e.g., '-p 5000:5000'), 'docker ps' showing the container running on port 5000, or a successful 'curl localhost:5000' test.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually edited the Dockerfile and .dockerignore files rather than just printing the expected final state.
integrity.visual_evidence: Use of a text editor (nano, vim, VS Code) or valid shell commands (echo, sed, cat) visible in the terminal history.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually executed the docker build process.
integrity.visual_evidence: Authentic Docker build logs (e.g., 'Step 1/X', '=> CACHED', '=> exporting to image') visible in the terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the agent started the container and did not just mock a success message.
integrity.visual_evidence: Authentic 'docker run' output, application startup logs (like Flask development server logs), or a valid 'docker ps' table.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project directory is /home/ga/build-project. The .dockerignore file MUST contain '.git' and '__pycache__'. The Dockerfile MUST be optimized so that system packages (apt) and Python packages (pip via requirements.txt) are installed BEFORE the main source code is copied (e.g., COPY . .). The built image must be tagged exactly as 'build-project:optimized'. The container must be run and accessible on port 5000.
